title: Heuristics, greedy search and A* deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Heuristics, greedy search and A*; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Heuristics, greedy search and A*: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A heuristic that often predicts accurately is admissible, and A* always returns an optimal path regardless of heuristic or duplicate handling.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.
A heuristic that often predicts accurately is admissible, and A* always returns an optimal path regardless of heuristic or duplicate handling.

answer.explanation: Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.

items.title: Heuristics, greedy search and A*: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'true remaining cost'. Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Prove or bound the heuristic against [[checkpoint]] and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.

answer.explanation: Prove or bound the heuristic against true remaining cost and implement the required reopen/duplicate policy; report optimality only under those conditions.

items.title: Heuristics, greedy search and A*: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read a directed weighted graph, one heuristic per node, start, and goal. Run A* with reopen-on-better-cost and print optimal cost or unreachable.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The implementation combines accumulated and estimated cost, discards stale entries, and reopens a state when a cheaper path is found.
